channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Middle East latest: US envoy arrives in Lebanon to meet with officials about possible cease-fire
item.description: A U.S. envoy has arrived in Beirut to meet with Lebanese officials about a possible cease-fire in the Israel-Hezbollah war. Amos Hochstein, a senior advisor to United States President Joe Biden, arrived Tuesday, a day after Hezbollah reportedly gave a positive response to a U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Concerns grow for imprisoned Nobel Peace Prize laureate Narges Mohammadi's health in Iran
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Imprisoned Nobel Peace Prize laureate Narges Mohammadi underwent a complex surgery in Iran that saw part of a bone in her right leg removed over cancer fears but was immediately returned to prison, raising the risks to her life, rights groups warned.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Bear shot, killed by homeowner in Southern California
item.description: A California man is being criticized after he shot and killed a bear in his yard last week that he claimed was getting into his chicken coop.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Pennsylvania's high court orders counties not to count disputed ballots in US Senate race
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — Pennsylvania's state Supreme Court on Monday weighed in on a flashpoint amid ongoing vote counting in the U.S. Senate election between Democratic Sen.
item.dc:creator: MARC LEVY, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: 3 people are killed in random stabbings in New York City. A suspect is in custody
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A man fatally stabbed three people across a swath of Manhattan on Monday morning, carrying out a series of random attacks without uttering a word to his victims, officials said.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: TSA intercepts loaded handgun found in flight crew member's carry-on at DC-area airport
item.description: TSA officers at Reagan National Airport in Arlington, Virginia, intercepted a loaded 9mm handgun from a flight crew member's carry-on baggage on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Riders suspended in air on Knott's Berry Farm ride
item.description: Video showed what appeared to be the Sol Spin, which sends riders up to six stories in the air as they rotate independently on one of six spinning arms.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, NewsNation

item.title: Agent says GPS data puts Georgia student Laken Riley in same area as man accused in her death
item.description: ATHENS, Ga. (AP) — An FBI special agent testified Monday that electronic location data seems to place Georgia nursing student Laken Riley and the man accused of killing her in the same wooded area at the time of her death.
item.dc:creator: KATE BRUMBACK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Sister of ex-Marine charged in subway chokehold death testifies to his 'calm' spirit and patriotism
item.description: Defense attorneys began their defense of a former U.S. Marine charged with fatally choking a homeless man aboard a Manhattan subway by calling the defendant's older sister to the witness stand.
item.dc:creator: Jake Offenhartz, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Federal appeals court won't block upcoming nitrogen gas execution in Alabama
item.description: MONTGOMERY, Ala. (AP) — A federal appeals court said Monday it will let the nation’s third execution with nitrogen gas go forward this week in Alabama, rejecting arguments that the new method causes unconstitutional levels of pain. The 11th U.S.
item.dc:creator: KIM CHANDLER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Suspect in custody after 'unprovoked' New York City stabbing spree leaves 3 people dead: police
item.description: Two men were killed and a woman left fighting for her life after a homeless New York City man allegedly committed a series of random stabbings, police said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: India overtakes China as top source of international students at US colleges
item.description: India has overtaken China as the top source of international students for U.S. colleges, according to a report from Open Doors.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KRON4

item.title: 1 in 5 get news from social media influencers: Pew poll
item.description: Sixty-five percent of those who get news from social media say influencers have bettered their understanding of current events and civic issues.
item.dc:creator: Joanne Haner, Fox 5

item.title: Trump taps Brendan Carr to be FCC chairman
item.description: President-elect Trump is tapping Brendan Carr of the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) to lead the agency.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, WGN 9

item.title: From the Amazon rainforest, Biden declares nobody can reverse US progress on clean energy
item.description: MANAUS, Brazil (AP) — Speaking from the Amazon rainforest, President Joe Biden declared Sunday that there’s no going back in America’s “clean energy revolution” even as the incoming Trump administration vows to spur fossil fuel production and scale back efforts against climate change.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, GABRIELA SÁ PESSOA and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: 3 hurt when plane crashes near Southern California dragstrip hosting racing event
item.description: At least two parked vehicles on the ground were struck when the small plane crashed around near the dragstrip.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Coast Guard detains more than 20 migrants on boat off California coast
item.description: The Coast Guard apprehended 21 migrants, including 18 from Mexico, one from Russia and two from Uzbekistan, off the coast of Newport Beach, California, last week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pollster Ann Selzer steps aside in wake of huge Iowa poll miss
item.description: Top pollster Ann Selzer is set to retire from the Des Moines Register’s Iowa Poll after a shocking presidential survey was so wrong.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Elon Musk is hiring for DOGE. Here's the type of people he wants to recruit
item.description: Just days after being tapped to co-lead the Department of Government Efficiency, Elon Musk is searching for "high-IQ" individuals to join the taskforce.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, Fox 5

item.title: An Israeli strike in Beirut kills Hezbollah's spokesman, while a strike in Gaza kills at least 30
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — A rare Israeli strike in central Beirut killed the Hezbollah militant group's chief spokesman on Sunday, while an Israeli strike in northern Gaza ’s Beit Lahiya killed at least 30 people, a hospital director there told The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: FADI TAWIL, BASSEM MROUE and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Longest-waiting child in Arkansas foster care system finds forever family
item.description: After more than 6,100 days in the Arkansas foster care system, 17-year-old Jose has now found his forever family.
item.dc:creator: Mattison Gafner, WGN 9

item.title: 8-year-old who raised money for mother's headstone now giving back
item.description: Emouree Johnson, 8, ran a lemonade stand earlier this year to raise money for a headstone for her mother. Now, she's raising funds to help other kids.
item.dc:creator: Josiah Elmore, Fox 5

item.title: How this duct-taped banana came to be worth $1 million
item.description: Walk into any supermarket and you can generally buy a banana for less than $1. But a banana duct-taped to a wall? That might sell for more than $1 million at an upcoming auction.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: US students who box, skydive and help youth and asylum seekers are among 2025's Rhodes scholars
item.description: A group of 32 students from the United States have been selected to attend the University of Oxford as part of the prestigious Rhodes scholar program in the coming year among an international class representing more than 70 nations, scholarship officials announced.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN P. D. HANNON and JOHN HANNA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: These are the worst passwords you can have in 2024
item.description: Don't rely on easy-to-remember passwords too much — because all of us tend to think more alike than we'd think.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, KTLA 5

item.title: McDonald's is investing $100 million to bring customers back after E. coli outbreak
item.description: McDonald's is investing $100 million to bring customers back to stores after an outbreak of E. coli food poisoning tied to onions on the fast-food giant's Quarter Pounder hamburgers. The investments include $65 million that will go directly to the hardest-hit franchises, the company said. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Man arrested for allegedly trying to smuggle hundreds of tarantulas and centipedes on flight
item.description: A South Korean man was arrested in Peru for allegedly attempting to smuggle hundreds of tarantulas and centipedes on a flight back to Korea.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Olav Thon, billionaire Norwegian real estate developer, dead at 101
item.description: Olav Thon, a billionaire entrepreneur recognizable for his bright red cap who went from selling leather and fox hides in his youth to build one of Norway's biggest real estate empires, has died, his company said Saturday. He was 101.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Nuggets star Nikola Jokic and coach Michael Malone miss game against Pelicans for personal reasons
item.description: The Denver Nuggets were without star center Nikola Jokic and coach Michael Malone for their NBA Cup opener Friday night against the New Orleans Pelicans.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Southwest flight evacuated after cell phone catches fire
item.description: Passengers of a Southwest Airlines flight sitting at a Denver International Airport gate had to evacuate after a cell phone battery caught on fire.
item.dc:creator: Morgan Whitley, WFLA, NBC

item.title: California man sentenced to life for hate crime in the killing of a gay college student
item.description: SANTA ANA, Calif. (AP) — A California man convicted of stabbing to death a gay University of Pennsylvania student in an act of hate was sentenced Friday to life without parole in prison.
item.dc:creator: AMY TAXIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump names Karoline Leavitt as White House press secretary
item.description: Leavitt, 27, previously served in the Trump White House as assistant press secretary under Kayleigh McEnany, now a leading host on Fox News.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trial begins for the man accused of killing Georgia nursing student Laken Riley
item.description: ATHENS, Ga. (AP) — A Venezuelan man "went hunting for females on the University of Georgia’s campus” earlier this year and ended up killing nursing student Laken Riley after a struggle, a prosecutor said Friday.
item.dc:creator: KATE BRUMBACK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Transgender-rights advocates say the election of Trump and his allies marks a major setback
item.description: Election victories for Donald Trump and other candidates whose campaigns demeaned transgender people reinforced a widespread backlash against trans rights. For America's LGBTQ-rights movement, it adds up to one of the most sustained setbacks in its history.
item.dc:creator: DAVID CRARY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: What happens next in The Onion's effort to buy Alex Jones' Infowars
item.description: The Onion’s winning bid for Alex Jones ’ Infowars platform is under review by a federal bankruptcy judge after Jones and his lawyers complained about how an auction was conducted.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: As Trump picks Florida men for top jobs, new political possibilities open up in the Sunshine State
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (AP) — As President-elect Donald Trump names picks for his new administration from his private club in Palm Beach and prepares to return to the White House, he’s bringing the Sunshine State with him. With Sen. Marco Rubio tapped to be the next secretary of state, Rep.
item.dc:creator: KATE PAYNE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Fake nurse worked at multiple hospitals, police say
item.description: Police have arrested a woman who they say has been posing as a nurse and working at hospitals throughout Los Angeles County without a license.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, WFLA, NBC
